---
title: 'Add an image source'
output: rmarkdown::html_document
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```


## Adding an image source

There are two ways to add an image source to your map:

### The add_image() function

Using the `add_image()` function, you can add an image source by passing the image URL or file path directly to the `url` argument.

You can pass either a `map`or `mapProxy` object to `add_image()`.

```{r add-source-map}
library(toro)

map() |>
  add_image(id = "leaf-icon", url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/0/02/Leaf_icon.png/600px-Leaf_icon.png") |>
  add_symbol_layer(id = "quakes_circles", source = "quakes_data", hover_column = "description")
```


### Adding the source directly in the layer function

Alternatively, you can add a GeoJSON source directly within a layer function by passing a sf object or GeoJSON string to the `source` argument.
In this case the source ID will be automatically generated by appending the layer ID to `source-`. For example, if you add a layer with `id = "my_layer"` and `source` is a sf object, the source will be added to the map with the ID `"source-my_layer"`.
This allows you to add the source and layer in a single step, without needing to call `add_source()` separately.


```{r advanced-map}
library(toro)

map() |>
  add_circle_layer(id = "quakes_circles", source = quakes_data, hover_column = "description")
```
